mnemonics: D.C. stands for 'Doesn't Count' in a Karnaugh Map. You can group them as 1s or 0s to make the largest possible groups and get a simpler equation.
keyPoints: Don't-care conditions are input combinations that will never occur or whose output is irrelevant.
They are represented by an 'X' in a truth table or Karnaugh map.
They can be treated as either a 1 or a 0 to help minimize the logic function.
commonMistakes: Treating all don't-cares as 1s automatically, instead of strategically choosing their value to form the largest groups.
Forgetting that the simplified function only needs to match the specified minterms; its output for don't-care inputs can be arbitrary.
